cards.prompt: A task from a TAP/async method is hot (Status != Created); calling Start() on it throws InvalidOperationException
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; async Task<int> Tap(){ await Task.Delay(1); return 1; } var hot = Tap(); bool started = hot.Status != TaskStatus.Created; string err="none"; try { hot.Start(); } catch (InvalidOperationException){ err="InvalidOperationException"; } await hot; Console.WriteLine($"hot={started} start={err}");

cards.prompt: An async method runs synchronously until its first await: A (pre-await) then B (caller) then C (resume) => ABC
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; async Task Work(){ Console.Write("A"); await Task.Delay(10); Console.Write("C"); } var t = Work(); Console.Write("B"); await t; Console.WriteLine();

cards.prompt: The three completed task statuses RanToCompletion / Faulted / Canceled all have IsCompleted == true
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading; using System.Threading.Tasks; var ok = Task.FromResult(1); var fault = Task.FromException(new Exception("x")); var canc = Task.FromCanceled(new CancellationToken(true)); Console.WriteLine($"{ok.Status} {fault.Status} {canc.Status} | completed={ok.IsCompleted && fault.IsCompleted && canc.IsCompleted}");
